metadata.title: Database Schema Migration - Add ai_context JSONB Column

implementation_phase.objective: Add a JSONB column named `ai_context` to the `test_results` table to store structured failure context that enables AI-powered troubleshooting and queryable failure analysis.

context.current_state: The `test_results` table already has a JSONB `logs` column (added in migration 003). This establishes a pattern for JSONB column addition that we follow here.

ai_context_schema.description: The ai_context column stores a structured JSON object optimized for AI consumption. Only populated for failed/timedout tests. NULL for passed/skipped tests.
ai_context_schema.json_structure: { "test_id": "string", // Format: {file}::{test_name} "timestamp": "ISO-8601", // When failure occurred "file": "string", // Relative path to test file "suite": ["string"], // Describe block hierarchy "test": "string", // Test title "error": { "message": "string", // Error message "type": "string", // TimeoutError, AssertionError, etc. "location": "string" // file:line:column from stack }, "steps": ["string"], // Last N test steps (from Playwright) "last_step": "string", // Step where it failed "duration_ms": "number", // Test duration "retries": "number", // Retry count "last_api": { // Last API call (if available) "method": "string", "url": "string", "status": "number" }, "page_url": "string", // URL at failure time "browser": "string" // Browser name }

step.title: Create Migration File
Run Migration
step.commands:
cd /Users/agustinmontoya/Attorneyshare/e2e-test-dashboard
# Run migration against Neon database # Replace with your actual database connection method psql $DATABASE_URL -f scripts/008_add_ai_context.sql
# Or if using a migration runner # npm run db:migrate

step.title: Verify Migration
step.commands:
-- Verify column exists SELECT column_name, data_type, column_default FROM information_schema.columns WHERE table_name = 'test_results' AND column_name = 'ai_context';
-- Verify indexes exist SELECT indexname, indexdef FROM pg_indexes WHERE tablename = 'test_results' AND indexname LIKE '%ai_context%';

example_queries.description: Once the column is populated, these queries become possible:
example_queries.query: SELECT test_name, test_file, ai_context->'error'->>'message' as error FROM test_results WHERE ai_context->'error'->>'type' = 'TimeoutError' ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 20;

query.name: Find tests that failed on specific step

example_queries.query: SELECT test_name, ai_context->>'last_step' as failed_step FROM test_results WHERE ai_context->>'last_step' LIKE '%click%' AND status = 'failed';
SELECT test_name, ai_context->'last_api' as api_info FROM test_results WHERE ai_context->'last_api'->>'url' LIKE '%graphql%' AND (ai_context->'last_api'->>'status')::int >= 400;

success_criteria.criterion: Column type is JSONB with NULL default
GIN index exists for efficient JSONB queries

implementation_phase.next_phase: After this migration, proceed to Phase 2 to update the dashboard-reporter to populate this column and export local JSON files.
